findings.description: Window size is inferred from model family capability, not actual active context limit. For Sonnet/Opus sessions without 1M activation, this can overstate window size (1M vs 200k), under-report utilization, and delay checkpoint triggers.

The new global config override (checkpoint.window_size) is not applied here, while it is applied in user-prompt-submit. This creates inconsistent checkpoint behavior across hooks for the same session/config.
Like post-tool-use, this hook calls detectWindowSize without passing checkpoint.window_size override, so window-size selection is inconsistent with user-prompt-submit.
Hot-path code now performs two full transcript reads (detectWindowSize and readTokenGauge) on every tool-use event. This increases I/O overhead and may affect hook latency on large transcripts.
